extracted_operations.name: retry-counter-in-state
extracted_operations.trigger: Any cyclic LM workflow (tool-call -> reflect -> retry, agent self-critique, plan-act-observe).
extracted_operations.action: Add an explicit integer counter to the workflow's persistent state (LangGraph TypedDict field with operator.add reducer, CrewAI shared context dict, app-side dict for Claude/OpenAI SDKs). Increment in the body. Exit when counter >= N.
extracted_operations.output: A bounded loop with deterministic termination after N iterations regardless of LM behaviour.

framework_mapping.langgraph: state['retries']: Annotated[int, operator.add] + conditional edge -> END
framework_mapping.crewai: shared dict in Crew context or Flow state; check before each Task

extracted_operations.name: stagnation-detection
extracted_operations.trigger: Loop is bounded by counter but each iteration is repeating itself (same tool args, same error message, same generated diff).
extracted_operations.action: Store the previous iteration's distinguishing artifact (last_error, last_tool_call_hash, last_output_hash) in state. Before incrementing the counter, compare with previous; if equal, exit immediately with status 'stagnation' rather than burning N iterations on identical work.
extracted_operations.output: Fast-fail on infinite loops that the counter alone would only catch after N expensive iterations.

framework_mapping.langgraph: state['last_error']: str | None + conditional edge checks equality
framework_mapping.crewai: task callback that hashes output; stored in Crew context

extracted_operations.name: human-or-outer-loop-escalation
extracted_operations.action: Do not raise / crash. Route to a designated escalation node: LangGraph node that calls interrupt(), CrewAI Task with human_input=True, Claude tool call that surfaces to user, or app-level fallback path. The escalation node must include the loop's final state (counter, last_error, partial output) so a human or outer agent can diagnose.
extracted_operations.output: A graceful 'give up and ask' branch with full context preserved.

framework_mapping.claude_sdk: tool that messages a human channel

extracted_operations.name: token-and-wall-clock-budget
extracted_operations.trigger: Each iteration is itself unbounded in cost (long context, web-search, code execution).
extracted_operations.action: In addition to iteration counter, enforce a token budget (sum of input+output tokens) and a wall-clock timeout at the outer caller. The counter handles 'how many times'; the budget handles 'how much per time'. Either limit exhausted -> escalation (OP-3).
extracted_operations.output: Defense in depth: no single iteration can blow the budget even if the counter has not yet expired.

framework_mapping.langgraph: track cumulative tokens in state; check in conditional edge

extracted_operations.name: progress-witness-per-iteration
extracted_operations.action: Identify the state field that MUST change between iterations to prove progress (a new error message, a refined plan, a different SQL query, a higher confidence score). Encode it as a typed state field. Make the conditional edge verify the witness changed; if it didn't, treat as stagnation (OP-2).
extracted_operations.output: Loop body that is structurally incapable of being a no-op.

framework_mapping.openai_agents: tool_choice + function schema enforces non-empty delta

extracted_operations.name: never-raise-the-safety-net
extracted_operations.trigger: User asks 'just raise recursion_limit / max_iter to make the error go away.'
extracted_operations.action: Refuse. Instead diagnose: (a) is there a counter? (b) is there a witness? (c) is the LM seeing the previous error so it can adapt? Patch those. The safety net stays low (LangGraph default 25, CrewAI default 20) precisely because it is a circuit breaker, not a control knob.
extracted_operations.output: A diagnostic conversation that ends with adding OP-1+OP-2 instead of moving the failure further away.

framework_mapping.langgraph: leave recursion_limit at default; add state counter
framework_mapping.crewai: leave Agent.max_iter at sane default; root-cause delegation loop
framework_mapping.claude_sdk: leave max_tokens at sane default; bound app-side loop
framework_mapping.openai_agents: leave max_turns at sane default; bound Run config

anti_patterns: Raising recursion_limit / max_iter / max_turns without root-causing the loop
Letting the LM self-terminate via 'should I stop?' reflection
Bounding only by token cost — slow loops never hit it
Per-agent max_iter when delegation crosses agents (CrewAI gotcha)
Bounding without an escalation path — graph just crashes with a 500
cross_framework_axes: where the counter lives (state field vs config vs app code)
how the exit is expressed (conditional edge vs return vs stop_reason)
what happens when the bound is hit (interrupt vs exception vs message)
